text:
There once was a boy named Jack. He lived with his family in a cozy little town. One summer, Jack climbed the ancient oak tree in their backyard. After dangling carefully for a while, he reached the low branch he wanted.

Suddenly, he noticed something peculiar. A shiny coin lay nestled in an old walnut tree branch nearby. He pulled it out. It was a gold nugget! Jack's heart pounds—he couldn't believe his eyes. He scampered down the tree.

His mother discovered the priceless lump of gold. "Jack!" she exclaimed. "That must be worth a fortune!"

But Jack didn't want to sell it. How could he leave something so beautiful fluttering in a tree? He pondered, feeling guilty.

And then an idea struck him. Why not leave it here? He'd find it again someday.

He buried it deep in the forest floor.

Years went by.

Jack heard tales of old fortune hunters wandering these woods. He decided this nugget was his
